seoTitle: Paraguay Residency by Investment | Investor Pass
seoDescription: Get permanent residency in Paraguay through investment from USD 70,000. Routes to residency and a path to citizenship by naturalisation. Guidance from Lincoln.

faqs.question: What is the Paraguay Investor Pass?
faqs.answer: It is Paraguay's investment-linked residency framework, established by Resolution N° 0283/2026. A qualifying investment through an approved route gives you access to the Foreign Investor Certificate (CIE) and the permanent residency process — and, after the constitutional residence period, the right to apply for citizenship by naturalisation.

faqs.question: How much do I need to invest?
faqs.answer: Resolution 0283/2026 sets four routes: a company capitalised through SUACE from USD 70,000, approved tourism projects from USD 150,000, real estate from USD 200,000, and financial instruments from USD 200,000. A separate, pre-existing pathway via Paraguay's audiovisual institute allows residency through a film-production contribution from approximately USD 40,000. We help you choose the route that matches your strategy, not just your budget.

faqs.question: Is the Foreign Investor Certificate the same as residency?
faqs.answer: No. The MIC must issue the Foreign Investor Certificate (CIE) within five business days of a complete application, but it is a distinct investor credential. Permanent residency is a separate status granted by the migration authority, with its own process and timeline. We keep the two clearly apart — and so does the law.

faqs.question: When can I apply for Paraguayan citizenship?
faqs.answer: Paraguay's Constitution (Art. 148) permits an application for naturalisation after three years of permanent residence, subject to language, civics, and physical-presence requirements, with further administrative processing time. Citizenship is granted through naturalisation — it is not conferred by the investment itself.

faqs.question: Will Paraguay tax my worldwide income?
faqs.answer: Paraguay applies a territorial tax system, so income arising outside Paraguay is generally outside the scope of Paraguayan income tax. Legal residency and tax residency are separate concepts: holding an Investor Pass does not by itself determine where you are tax resident. We assess that individually, within your wider structure.

faqs.question: Can I include my family?
faqs.answer: Family members can generally be included in an Investor Pass application. The specifics depend on your circumstances and the route chosen, and are confirmed before anything is committed.

blocks.eyebrow: República del Paraguay · Residency by Investment
blocks.lead: A single qualifying investment grants direct permanent residency in one of South America's fastest-growing economies. Established under Resolution N° 0283/2026, the Investor Pass bypasses the temporary-residency stage entirely — with a clear pathway, in time, to citizenship by naturalisation.

paragraphs.text: The Investor Pass is Paraguay's investment-linked route to direct permanent residency. A qualifying investment is certified by the Foreign Investor Certificate — the Constancia de Inversionista Extranjero (CIE) — after which the permanent residency application proceeds with the national migration authority, without the usual two-year temporary stage.

Citizenship is a separate, later step: after the constitutional period of residence, a permanent resident may apply for citizenship by naturalisation. It is part of the journey — but residency is the destination most investors are buying.

lawcite.label: The law · quoted
lawcite.body: The Investor Pass is established by Resolution N° 0283/2026 of Paraguay's Ministry of Industry and Commerce (MIC), signed on 21 April 2026 in coordination with the Dirección Nacional de Migraciones. It rebuilds the framework for the Constancia de Inversionista Extranjero (CIE), abrogating the earlier Resolution N° 1052/2025, and grants foreign investors direct access to permanent residency.

asideNotice.title: Read this first
asideNotice.body: Three credentials are often confused. The Foreign Investor Certificate (CIE) is a fast investor credential, issued by the MIC within five business days of a complete application. Permanent residency is a separate status granted by the migration authority, typically over several months. Citizenship comes later still, by naturalisation. They are not the same thing.

asideTable.label: Investor certificate
asideTable.value: CIE — issued by the MIC within ~5 business days

asideTable.label: Maintenance
asideTable.value: At least one visit every three years

blocks.heading: Among the fastest-growing economies in South America.
blocks.lead: This is not a frontier bet. Paraguay reached investment grade with Moody's in 2024 (Baa3) and S&P in 2025 (BBB-) — and every comparable market that crossed that line saw a decade of sustained property appreciation. The Investor Pass is simply the door in.

facts.label: Average real GDP growth over the past decade — 2025 exceeded 6%
Flat 10% corporate, personal and VAT rates

blocks.heading: A property market in a once-in-a-generation phase.

paragraphs.text: Asunción's skyline is being rewritten. Paraguay's tallest building, Petra Tower (172m), topped out in 2025; Petra Imperiale is planned to reach 250m; and developers like CIVIS are delivering 50-storey towers in Las Lomas. Behind the towers is real demand in a fast-urbanising capital.

For investors, the appeal is clean. Gross rental yields in Asunción typically run 6–8% for residential and up to around 12% for commercial or mixed-use, in USD — in a territorial, dollar-friendly system where foreigners buy freely, on a passport alone.

asideTable.label: Income tax
asideTable.value: 10% flat — among the lowest in the region

asideNotice.title: The real estate route
asideNotice.body: Acquiring qualifying income-generating property from USD 200,000 satisfies the Investor Pass real estate track — see the developers and projects on the Real Estate page.

blocks.heading: Choose the route that fits your strategy — not just your budget.
blocks.lead: Each route leads into the same residency framework. They differ in capital, control, and what you actually own at the end.

rungs.title: Eligible Real Estate
rungs.text: Qualifying income-generating property — a tangible, appreciating asset you hold and control.

rungs.title: Financial Instruments
rungs.text: A qualifying financial investment held for the required period — the passive, hands-off route.

rungs.title: Approved Touristic Projects
rungs.text: Investment into approved tourism developments — yield potential plus residency.

rungs.title: SUACE Business Investment
rungs.text: Establish and capitalise a Paraguayan company through the unified business system.

rungs.title: Film Cultural Investment
rungs.text: A qualifying contribution to Paraguayan audiovisual production — a separate, lower-cost pathway.

blocks.note: The first four routes are the qualifying options under Resolution 0283/2026. The film contribution is a separate, pre-existing pathway via Paraguay's audiovisual institute (INAP), included here for completeness.

blocks.heading: What makes the residency genuinely worth holding.

cards.title: Territorial taxation
cards.text: Income generated outside Paraguay is generally outside the scope of local income tax, and the headline rate is a flat 10%. Legal residency and tax residency remain separate questions — but the underlying model is among the most rational in the region.

cards.title: Low cost of establishment
cards.text: The cost of living and doing business is a fraction of comparable jurisdictions, and qualifying property can be filed once at least 30% of the declared value is paid. Capital goes further here than almost anywhere in the Americas.

cards.title: Direct, durable residency
cards.text: The Investor Pass grants permanent residency directly — no two-year temporary stage — and it is maintained with at least one visit every three years. Residency you hold, not residency you rent.

cards.title: Southern Cone position
cards.text: Paraguay sits at the centre of Mercosur, bordered by Brazil and Argentina. It is the strategic anchor of a wider regional footprint — your foothold in a continent of options.

cards.title: Stable and open
cards.text: A stable currency, investment-grade ratings, an open posture toward foreign investment, and recognition of dual citizenship in defined circumstances. Paraguay does not punish you for being international.

cards.title: A citizenship pathway
cards.text: And, in time, optionality: after the qualifying period of permanent residence, eligibility to apply for citizenship by naturalisation. A later step — but a real one.

blocks.heading: For those who want optionality before they need it.

cards.title: The diversifier
cards.text: Americans, Europeans, and Australians who want a credible second base outside their home jurisdiction — before circumstances force the question.

cards.title: The entrepreneur
cards.text: Founders and operators who want a low-friction company, a territorial tax footing, and a foothold in Mercosur to build from.

cards.title: The citizenship-minded
cards.text: Families building deliberately toward a second passport, who understand that the strongest position is the one secured early.

blocks.pull: Residency by investment in the Republic of Paraguay.
blocks.lead: Request guidance on the Investor Pass and the route best suited to your circumstances.
